The **Svitavy Uplands** or **Svitavy Hills** ([Czech](/wiki/Czech_language "Czech language"): *Svitavská pahorkatina*) are [uplands](/wiki/Highland "Highland") and a [geomorphological mesoregion of the Czech Republic](/wiki/Geomorphological_division_of_the_Czech_Republic "Geomorphological division of the Czech Republic"). It is located in the [Pardubice Region](/wiki/Pardubice_Region "Pardubice Region") and it belongs to the largest mesoregions in the country.

## Geomorphology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Svitavy_Uplands&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geomorphology")]

The Svitavy Uplands is a [mesoregion](/wiki/Mesoregion_(geomorphology) "Mesoregion (geomorphology)") of the [East Bohemian Table](/w/index.php?title=East_Bohemian_Table&action=edit&redlink=1 "East Bohemian Table (page does not exist)") within the [Bohemian Massif](/wiki/Bohemian_Massif "Bohemian Massif"). It is a rugged hilly area with [highlands](/wiki/Highland "Highland") in the eastern part. It has a relatively uniform relief with [synclines](/wiki/Syncline "Syncline"), low ridges, [cuestas](/wiki/Cuesta "Cuesta"), valleys and furrows. The relief is complemented by [Pleistocene](/wiki/Pleistocene "Pleistocene") river terraces of the [Chrudimka](/wiki/Chrudimka "Chrudimka") River.[[1]](#cite_note-nm-1) The uplands are further subdivided into the microregions of Česká Třebová Highlands, Loučná Table and Chrudim Table.[[2]](#cite_note-upol-2)

There are a lot of medium-high hills. The highest peaks are located in the southern part of the territory. The highest peaks of the Svitavy Uplands are:

* Baldský vrch, 692 m (2,270 ft)
* Drašarov, 686 m (2,251 ft)
* Rohozná, 685 m (2,247 ft)
* Poličský vrch, 672 m (2,205 ft)
* Roh, 660 m (2,170 ft)
* Modřecký vrch, 657 m (2,156 ft)
* U Mariánského obrazu, 654 m (2,146 ft)
* Na drahách, 649 m (2,129 ft)
* Mladějovský vrch, 647 m (2,123 ft)
* Mirand, 640 m (2,100 ft)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Svitavy_Uplands&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The Svitavy Uplands roughly stretches from the south to the northwest and north. The uplands have an area of 1,692 square kilometres (653 sq mi) and an average elevation of 412 metres (1,352 ft).[[2]](#cite_note-upol-2)

There are several rivers, but in general the area is poor in smaller watercourses. The main [European watershed](/wiki/European_watershed "European watershed") passes through the Svitavy Uplands. The river [Svitava](/wiki/Svitava_(river) "Svitava (river)") and its tributaries in the southern tip of the uplands head to the [Black Sea](/wiki/Black_Sea "Black Sea"). The northern part with the rivers [Chrudimka](/wiki/Chrudimka "Chrudimka"), [Loučná](/wiki/Lou%C4%8Dn%C3%A1_(river) "Loučná (river)"), Novohradka, Třebovka and [Tichá Orlice](/wiki/Tich%C3%A1_Orlice "Tichá Orlice") is drained by the [Elbe](/wiki/Elbe "Elbe").[[1]](#cite_note-nm-1)

Suitable natural conditions contributed to the creation of many settlements in the Svitavy Uplands. The most populated towns in the territory are [Chrudim](/wiki/Chrudim "Chrudim"), [Svitavy](/wiki/Svitavy "Svitavy"), [Česká Třebová](/wiki/%C4%8Cesk%C3%A1_T%C5%99ebov%C3%A1 "Česká Třebová"), [Ústí nad Orlicí](/wiki/%C3%9Ast%C3%AD_nad_Orlic%C3%AD "Ústí nad Orlicí"), [Vysoké Mýto](/wiki/Vysok%C3%A9_M%C3%BDto "Vysoké Mýto"), [Litomyšl](/wiki/Litomy%C5%A1l "Litomyšl"), [Přelouč](/wiki/P%C5%99elou%C4%8D "Přelouč") and [Polička](/wiki/Poli%C4%8Dka "Polička").

## Geology and pedology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Svitavy_Uplands&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geology and pedology")]

The geologically diverse bedrock is dominated by [marlite](/wiki/Marlite "Marlite"), [claystones](/wiki/Mudrock#Claystone "Mudrock"), [spongilites](/wiki/Spongilite "Spongilite"), [sandstones](/wiki/Sandstone "Sandstone") and marly limestones. The most widespread soils are [cambisol](/wiki/Cambisol "Cambisol") and [brown earth](/wiki/Brown_earth "Brown earth").[[1]](#cite_note-nm-1)

## Nature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Svitavy_Uplands&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Nature")]

The area is largely agricultural. Forest cover does not exceed 30%.[[1]](#cite_note-nm-1) There are basically no protected landscape areas in the territory, only a few small-scale protected areas. The most notable of them is the Rohová National Nature Reserve.
